title: SyncTools for Unity
description:
SyncTools for Unity

Overview

SyncTools Pro is a cross-software tool designed to simplify the process of copying and pasting detailed scene data between different applications. With just one click, you can export objects—including meshes, lights, cameras, materials, animations, and more—from one scene and import them into another. This saves you considerable time and effort in your production workflow.


Multi-DCC Platform Support

The SyncTools series now extends support to major digital content creation software, including:

  • ✅ SyncTools for Maya (Released)

  • ✅ SyncTools for 3ds Max (Released)

  • ✅ SyncTools for Houdini (Released)

  • ✅ SyncTools for Cinema 4D (Released)

  • ✅ SyncTools for Rhino (Released)

  • ✅ SyncTools for Marvelous Designer (Released)

  • ✅ SyncTools for Unity (Released)

  • ✅ SyncTools for Substance Painter (Released)

  • ✅ SyncTools for Unreal Engine (Released)

  • ✅ SyncTools for Zbrush (Released)

All plugins utilize a unified data exchange protocol, enabling lossless asset conversion across platforms and true multi-software collaborative workflows.



What SyncTools for Unity Does

SyncTools for Unity focuses on fast scene data exchange into and out of Unity. It is designed for workflows like:

  • Bringing layout / lighting / camera setups from DCC tools into Unity

  • Sending selected Unity scene objects to other DCC tools for refinement

  • Iterating quickly between Unity and external tools without rebuilding scenes manually

Note: SyncTools transfers supported scene data. Unity-specific components and behaviors may still require manual setup after import.

Unity Installation & Usage


1. Plugin Installation

Prerequisites

  • Unity Version: 2020.3 LTS and newer

    • Recommended: 2021.3+ / 2022.3+ / 2023.x

  • Required Package: FBX Exporter (com.unity.formats.fbx)

Install FBX Exporter Package

  1. Open Unity Editor → Window > Package Manager

  2. Click + (top-left) → Add package from git URL

  3. Enter: com.unity.formats.fbx

    • Or search FBX Exporter in Unity Registry and install

  4. Wait for installation to finish

Install SyncTools Plugin

  1. Copy the SyncTools folder into: Assets/

  2. Unity compiles scripts automatically; installation is complete

  3. You’ll find SyncTools in the Unity menu bar

2. Plugin Settings

Open Settings Window

  • Unity menu bar → SyncTools > Settings

Configuration Options

  • Cache Path
    Specifies the cache directory used for copy/paste exchange.

    • Default: Documents/cache

    • Must match the cache path used on Blender/Maya side

  • Import Folder
    Specifies where imported assets are stored in the Unity project.

    • Must start with Assets/

    • Default: Assets/SyncToolsCache/Incoming

  • Delete Cache Files after Import
    When enabled, automatically removes temporary cache files after paste completes.

    • Default: Enabled

  • Set Import Camera Near Clip
    When enabled, imported cameras will automatically apply the near clip plane value.

  • Near Clip Value
    Used when camera setting is enabled.

    • Default: 1.0

✅ Changes are automatically saved to Unity EditorPrefs.

3. Workflow

Copy

  1. Select the GameObjects you want to copy (Hierarchy or Scene View)

  2. Use SyncTools > Copy (Export Selected)

    • Shortcut: Ctrl+Shift+C (Windows) / Cmd+Shift+C (Mac)

  3. SyncTools exports your selection to the shared cache for use in other DCC software.

Paste

  1. Switch to your target scene

  2. Use SyncTools > Paste (Import Latest)

    • Shortcut: Ctrl+Shift+V (Windows) / Cmd+Shift+V (Mac)

  3. SyncTools imports the latest available cached data, applies settings (if enabled), and cleans up temporary files (if enabled).

✅ All operations support Unity Undo.

Version Compatibility

Official Support

  • Unity 2021.3 LTS / 2022.3 LTS / 2023.x

  • Blender 4.0+

Experimental

  • Unity 2020.3 LTS (some features may be limited)

Required Package

  • FBX Exporter (com.unity.formats.fbx)

    • Minimum: 7.1.0

    • Recommended: Latest stable

Data Compatibility

Full Support

  • ✔ Basic geometry (Mesh)

  • ✔ Transform hierarchies

  • ✔ Core material attributes (PBR workflow)

  • ✔ Point / Spot / Directional lights

  • ✔ Basic animation keyframes

  • ✔ Camera parameters (FOV / Focal Length)

Partial Support

  • ⚠ Complex shader nodes (auto-converted to Unity standard materials)

  • ⚠ Skeletal animation (requires matching Unity Avatar system)

  • ⚠ UV maps (supports standard UV channels)

  • ⚠ Normal maps (may require enabling in import settings)

Unsupported

  • ✖ Unity-specific components (Collider, Rigidbody, etc. — add manually)

  • ✖ Particle systems

  • ✖ Physics constraints

  • ✖ Custom shaders

  • ✖ Procedural content

Important Notes

1️⃣ Cache Path Synchronization
Ensure the Cache Path on Unity side matches the cache path used in Blender/Maya, otherwise exchange will fail.

2️⃣ FBX Exporter Dependency
The plugin depends on Unity’s FBX Exporter. If not installed, copy operations will fail with an installation prompt.

3️⃣ Import Folder Rules
Import Folder must start with Assets/, otherwise import will fail.

4️⃣ Naming
Special characters (e.g., @ # %) may cause import issues. Duplicate names are handled automatically.

5️⃣ Plugin Dependency
For best results, use matching SyncTools versions across supported DCC applications. (Check release notes for compatibility updates.)

Conclusion

SyncTools Pro provides a streamlined copy/paste workflow for transferring scene data into Unity from other DCC software. With simple controls and consistent settings, you can exchange detailed scene content efficiently and keep production moving.

Please let us know if you need any further assistance.

Support Blender Foundation

We believe in giving back to the community that makes our work possible. Every month, we dedicate a portion of our plugin's revenue to support the Blender Foundation. Thank you for helping us contribute to the future of open-source creativity.
documentation:
SyncTools Pro Installation & Usage Guide

📦 Installation

Unity Setup

Step 1: Install the FBX Exporter Package

  1. Open the Unity Editor

  2. Go to Window > Package Manager

  3. Click the + button (top-left) and choose Add package from git URL

  4. Enter: com.unity.formats.fbx

    • Or search FBX Exporter in Unity Registry and install it

  5. Wait for the installation to complete

Step 2: Install the SyncTools Plugin

  1. Copy the SyncTools folder into your Unity project’s Assets directory

  2. Unity will compile automatically. Once finished, you’ll see the SyncTools menu in the top menu bar

Step 3: Configure Settings

  1. Open SyncTools > Settings

  2. Set Cache Path

    • Default: Documents/cache

    • ⚠️ Important: Must match the cache path used in Blender/Maya

  3. Set Import Folder

    • Default: Assets/SyncToolsCache/Incoming

    • Must start with Assets/

🚀 Quick Start

Copy from Unity to Blender/Maya

  1. Select objects

    • In Unity, select the GameObjects you want to export in the Hierarchy

  2. Copy (Export Selected)

    • Menu: SyncTools > Copy (Export Selected)

    • Shortcut: Ctrl+Shift+C (Windows) or Cmd+Shift+C (Mac)

  3. Done

    • The exported data is saved to the cache directory

    • You can now paste/import on the Blender/Maya side

Paste from Blender/Maya into Unity

  1. Make sure the cache path matches

    • Confirm Unity’s Cache Path is the same as Blender/Maya’s

  2. Paste (Import Latest)

    • Menu: SyncTools > Paste (Import Latest)

    • Shortcut: Ctrl+Shift+V (Windows) or Cmd+Shift+V (Mac)

  3. Done

    • The latest cached content is imported and instantiated in the scene

    • Object names and hierarchy are automatically restored

⚙️ Settings Reference

Cache Path

  • Purpose: Stores cache files used for copy/paste exchange

  • Default: Documents/cache

  • Requirement: Must be the same path used in Blender/Maya

Import Folder

  • Purpose: Where imported assets are placed inside the Unity project

  • Default: Assets/SyncToolsCache/Incoming

  • Requirement: Must start with Assets/

Delete Cache Files After Import

  • Purpose: Automatically cleans up cache files after a successful paste

  • Default: Enabled

  • Recommendation: Keep enabled to prevent the cache folder from growing

Set Imported Camera Near Clip

  • Purpose: Automatically sets the near clipping plane for imported cameras

  • Default: Enabled, value 1.0

  • Use case: Helps avoid rendering issues caused by overly small near clip values in Unity

⌨️ Shortcuts

Action Windows Mac
Copy Ctrl+Shift+C Cmd+Shift+C
Paste Ctrl+Shift+V Cmd+Shift+V

Customize shortcuts: Unity menu Edit > Shortcuts

📋 Workflow Examples

Scenario 1: Unity → Blender

1. Unity: Select objects → Copy (Ctrl+Shift+C)
2. Blender: Open target scene → Paste operation
3. Done: Objects are imported into Blender

Scenario 2: Maya → Unity

1. Maya: Select objects → Copy operation
2. Unity: Open target scene → Paste (Ctrl+Shift+V)
3. Done: Objects are imported into the Unity scene

Scenario 3: Iteration Loop

1. Unity → Blender (edit materials)
2. Blender → Unity (preview results)
3. Unity → Blender (continue tweaking)
... repeat as needed

⚠️ Troubleshooting

Issue 1: Copy fails and says FBX Exporter is missing

Solution:

  • Verify the FBX Exporter package is installed

  • In Package Manager, search and install com.unity.formats.fbx

Issue 2: Paste fails and cannot find cache files

Solution:

  • Confirm the Cache Path matches Blender/Maya

  • Make sure you performed a copy/export on Blender/Maya first

  • Check that export_*.fbx files exist in the cache folder

Issue 3: Imported object names are incorrect

Solution:

  • Confirm hierarchy.txt exists in the cache directory

  • Check whether the file is locked by another process or corrupted

Issue 4: Materials are missing after import

Solution:

  • Check material/texture paths inside the file

  • Ensure texture files are in the expected directory

  • Inspect the FBX import Materials settings in Unity Inspector

📌 Important Notes

  1. ✅ Cache path must match across Unity, Blender, and Maya

  2. ✅ FBX Exporter is required on the Unity side

  3. ✅ Naming rules: avoid special characters (e.g., @#%)

  4. ✅ Version matching: use the same SyncTools version on all platforms

  5. ✅ Undo supported: all operations support Unity Undo (Ctrl+Z)

🔄 Version Compatibility

Unity Support

  • ✅ Official: 2021.3 LTS / 2022.3 LTS / 2023.x

  • ⚠️ Experimental: 2020.3 LTS

FBX Exporter

  • Minimum: 7.1.0

  • Recommended: Latest stable

📞 Getting Help

If you run into issues:

  • Check Unity’s Console for error messages

  • Confirm plugin versions match across all apps

  • Verify your cache path settings are correct

SyncTools Pro — making cross-software collaboration easier! 🎨🚀
